title: Make a Simple Stir-Fry
summary: Chop vegetables and protein, cook them quickly in a hot pan, and make a fast balanced meal.
learnerPromise: Throw vegetables and protein into a hot pan and make a quick meal.
whyItMatters: Stir-frying is one of the fastest ways to cook a full meal. It uses ingredients you probably already have and cleans up in one pan.
realLifeUses: Use up vegetables before they go bad
Make dinner in under 30 minutes with minimal cleanup
Build a meal around protein and vegetables you enjoy
Adapt the recipe based on what is in your kitchen
youWillLearn: How to chop vegetables and protein into similar-sized pieces
Why a hot pan matters for browning
The order to add ingredients so everything cooks evenly
How to season simply with oil, salt, and sauce
miniChallenge: Chop one vegetable and one protein, then cook them in a pan with oil.
steps: Wash your hands and gather one vegetable, one protein, oil, salt, and a pan.
Chop the vegetable and protein into small, similar-sized pieces so they cook evenly.
Heat a drizzle of oil in the pan over medium-high heat until it shimmers.
Add the protein first and cook until it is mostly done, then remove it from the pan.
Add the vegetables, cook for 3 to 5 minutes until they are tender but still have some bite, then return the protein to the pan and season with salt or a splash of soy sauce.
completionCriteria: Vegetables are tender but not mushy
commonProblems: Pan is not hot enough — food steams instead of browning; let the pan heat longer
Pieces are different sizes — some burn while others stay raw; chop more evenly next time
Too much food in the pan at once — cook in batches if needed
tips: Pat protein dry with a paper towel before cooking so it browns better
Cut vegetables while the pan is heating to save time
Keep a bowl nearby for scraps and packaging to keep your workspace tidy
helpBoundary: Ask for help if you are unsure how to use the stove. Oil can splatter at high heat.
